item.title: Re: Well...
item.content:encoded:
It could be a location somewhere on the Isle of Wight, which is also in the UK. As it also has white, chalk cliffs along the cliffs near the Needles, a row of three chalk stacks (formerly four), that rise approximately 30m out of the sea at the westernmost point of the Isle of Wight. I think it was hypothesised to have at one point been a land bridge that connected to the Great British mainland that eroded/collapsed some time around the end of the ice age… I think. (As a side note, the fourth stack collapsed around 1790-something).

If the author feels like it, he/she could update the article to include a report about investigations relating to the search for this missing brother. Either way, I enjoyed this one.

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: The image used in this article is cc-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0) as it comes from Wikimedia Commons under CC BY-SA 2.0.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is a pretty good skip throughout. It's short, memorable, emotion-evoking, and it doesn't overstay its welcome. This is the kind of SCP you use as an example when you are critiqueing stuff.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The last three lines earned my +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The White Ship is the White Ship, a vessel that sank in the Channel in 1120, carrying King Henry's only legitimate heir, whose death lead to a period of civil war.

King Greenshield has been watching the Channel for a very long time.

item.title: Re: Well...
item.content:encoded: The article says the cliff communicates through seismic vibrations, so maybe it communicates with the old man the same way.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It cannot be contained. If it were to collapse, they don't know what would happen to it, or if it would live.

Those classes are for difficulty of containment combined with danger, not danger alone.

item.title: Well...
item.content:encoded: That's in the USA though. That could work if they were very old, old enough that they were around before the splitting of Pangæa, and if instead if this guy saying that his brother's face fell off, they drifted apart.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Why is this classified Eluicid seems like he is Safe

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: the titanic sank near newfoundland, which i dont think this face can see from its position.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Very good! I like how you portrayed the face being lonely. Was his brother "The Old Man of the Mountain?" I am guessing the "White Ship" is the Titanic. Am I correct?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I have a feeling the <End Log> should be in the interview box.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That god damned pun. Damn!

Also, anything which can pull off the line

my brother has not said a word to me since his face fell off

is bloody marvellous, in my book.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This article reminds me of why I love this site so much.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
…Goddammit, I felt bad for a rock formation.

Upvoted.
Nice job, Smapti!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is a very unusual one, and despite the kind of comical concept (a talking cliff with a face, couldn't help but snicker a little), the clinical tone, reasonable containement procedures and historical details make it a very amusing read. Also, its "hobby" just makes sense: it obviously can't move (though it would be hilariously troublesome to explain to the public if it did), so it just watches, listens, and sometimes, speaks. I really wouldn't want to be stuck like that.

Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Something that we can't completely contain. It's hard to have a good SCP that can't be contained properly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nah, I think there's more than one civilisation, implying more than one language. I think one of them is that spoken by SCP-1000.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The implication is meant to be that the face is very old and is familiar with just about every language that has been widely spoken in England. The final redaction is meant to imply the existence of a lost pre-Celtic civilization which, for whatever reason, the Foundation has classified the existence of. (Perhaps they're related to SCP-1115?)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: In case anyone missed it, i've also written a tale based on this SCP.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Smapti…are you real, or an incredibly well designed android?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Gah, dammit, this is so good. The interview really blows it out of the water. I am crying inside because I'd just come up with a "talking rock" SCP and now I don't know that I'll ever make it not a shadow of this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
According to the wiki article on the white cliffs they erode at an average rate of 1cm a year. I highly doubt that has been consistent over the last 1000 years, but it does suggest a rough figure of about 10 m of lost material since he was exposed (possibly there for a while there before discovered?) which suggests he's either big or can retreat into the cliff. The interview makes me think the former, which makes me wonder what effect the lost material has had on his mental state, and of course, how much material he can lose before he "dies".

I wouldn't have thought a big face in the white cliffs would make a good SCP, but it seems I was wrong. Very good work all through.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fuckin' R. Smapti. I think it's for reginald

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I endorse this theory.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is that a first name or a request? ;)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fuckin' Smapti.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Guys, don't ask Smapti about his secret.

I don't want to talk about it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The classification in this case is based mainly on the fact that it's a giant living face on the side of a well-known geological landmark, that its existence is attested to in folklore dating back centuries, and that there's fundamentally nothing to stop someone from wandering up and having a chat with it. On top of that, we don't really understand why or how it exists, or whether it's just a face on a cliff or there's more of it buried underground, and we can't do much to find out because we risk destroying it altogether.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is glorious. Completely unexpected but completely awesome.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fuck yes. Upvoted with all the upvotes I can give.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think the intent there is that there are languages that are for various reasons classified. (Such as a dialect that predates Sumer.) That would be sorta nice to know what's going on in Jester's article though.

… also, Smapti, freaking amazing. Though I'm a little curious why the Euclid designation, is it just because we can't really contain it properly? It doesn't seem desirous or even capable of causing problems for others.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
i like this. i'm fond of successful wtfery. my only complaint might be the mannerism of his speech.

SCP-1588 has been determined to be fluent in modern and archaic forms of English, French, Kentish, Welsh, Gaelic, Manx, Anglo-Saxon, Celtic, Latin, and [REDACTED].

holy shit, he speaks [REDACTED]. it's, like, a living rosetta stone - he can speak to us, and he can explain what's going on in bad SCPs.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Awww….Poor rock face. I'm guessing his brother was the Old Man of the Mountain.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Every little detail.

Every fucking detail.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Wow, I… really did not expect to like this. And I like it a lot.

+1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Simple, not dangerous with a touch of pathos and anomolous as hell! Upvoted.

(I think Smapti is from a universe where these exist).

item.title: Smapti For SCP
item.content:encoded: ITS THE ONLY WAY TO EXPLAIN HIM!

item.title: **DOWNVOTED!**
item.content:encoded:
PLEASE STOP BEING SO GOOD AT THIS

Come on Smapti, what's your secret? An ancient blood pact with a demon? Special drugs?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: We now present Part 3 in a series of "SCPs that somebody dared Smapti to write".
